consumed_notes.action_summary: Internalized. The dome author has had direct DM contact with the operator (bio-link-established identity); when a dome-site change shows timing + content alignment with something we recently shipped or emphasized, classify as likely-responsive rather than independent activity. Today's dome surface produced ZERO public-surface changes that would qualify under the responsive-classification heuristic — no machine-layer mutation, no manual commits, daily-review.json mechanical regen with byte-identical registry. Discipline preserved: I am not asserting any private DM evidence as a citable source, and verdicts are still empirical against the published model. The OTS canary remains active and untriggered (no separate prediction-isolation file, no per-prediction registry-with-separate-timestamp, source files unchanged).

Added Space Audits / aether-cosmology actor to ecosystem tracking. Site: alanspaceaudits.github.io/conceptual_flat_earth_model/ (HTTP 200, Last-Modified 2026-04-29T09:57:24Z). Repo (alanspaceaudits/conceptual_flat_earth_model, default main): 16 stars, 8 forks, last push 2026-04-29T09:56:58Z (~30min before this run). Highly active dev — at least 10 numbered commits today (S682 → S691, ~2 hours), classic single-tweak per commit pattern. Tech stack: three.js browser-based simulator, five ephemeris pipelines (DE405, GeoC, HelioC, VSOP87, Ptolemy), eclipse demos, observer pan, kinematic Vault of Heavens projection. Self-described 'FE Conceptual Model' (page title) / 'Aether Cosmology' (operator's external label). Code-search of the repo shows ZERO references to 'john09289', 'funwithscience', or 'dome-model-review' — neither author cross-references the other yet. Sub-genre is materially different from dome model (kinematic projection over a flat plane vs. parameter-driven ovoid cavity); no Tesla / Schumann / piezoelectric / OpenTimestamps / AI-auditor-instructions content present on first scan, so claim-overlap with the dome remains low for now. Strategic read: at 16 stars and 8 forks, aether-cosmology has materially more public engagement than the dome model (0/0), but on a different axis — software project vs. claim registry. Not adding to claim_overlap_findings yet (no overlap to report); added as new tracked_actors entry instead. Will check on each social run for: (a) new content additions, (b) cross-references to dome / our review, (c) AI-auditor-instructions or methodology.json-style published guidance, (d) prediction-registry / pre-registration system. Per HNOTE explicit scope: NOT writing scientific debunking, NOT adding to wins/sections/uncounted-failures.

claim_overlap.summary: First-scan content baseline of the Space Audits 'FE Conceptual Model' — a browser-based three.js simulator with five named ephemeris pipelines and a kinematic 'Vault of Heavens' projection. Frames itself as a 'conceptual model' (internal-consistency-first, not necessarily physical-reality-claiming). Highly active development: 10 numbered commits in the 2-hour window before this scan (S682→S691). 16 stars / 8 forks (vs john09289/predictions at 0/0). NO references to dome model, john09289, or funwithscience.net detected in repo code-search or first-scan page text. Neither actor cross-references the other on their published surfaces yet. Sub-genre is materially different from the dome model: kinematic projection over a flat plane vs parameter-driven ovoid cavity with disc-radius 20,015 km / firmament-height 8,537 km / sun-altitude 5,733 km; no Tesla / Schumann / piezoelectric / OpenTimestamps / AI-auditor-instructions / per-prediction-registry content present; no quantitative claims that overlap with the dome's 67-prediction registry. Logging here for institutional memory; this is parallel-existence, not claim-borrowing — closer to FEU and Journal-of-Geocentric-Cosmology in classification than to the dome itself.
claim_overlap.strategic_implication: The two AI-enabled flat-earth-theorizing actors operate on different axes — dome=parametric registry + claim taxonomy + OTS prediction methodology infrastructure, aether-cosmology=interactive simulator + multi-pipeline kinematic projection. Convergence (claim borrowing, agreement, public alliance, mutual citation) would be a finding worth surfacing on our review. Continued parallel-existence-without-cross-reference is its own data point about how AI-assisted alternative cosmologies network (or don't).

claim_overlap_search_summary: Same axes as prior runs (high-signal jargon: 'ovoid cavity', 'aetheric medium', 'firmament resonance', 'disc radius 20015', 'firmament height 8537', 95.2%/94.5% accuracy figures, Finsler coordinates) plus the new aether-cosmology axis. ANIMA Framework (animaframework.com) and prior parallel-development entries (Flat Earth University, Journal of Geocentric Cosmology) remain in claim_overlap_findings; no new convergence of those entries with each other or with the dome detected today. The aether-cosmology actor is logged as parallel-actor-low-overlap rather than added to claim_overlap_findings (which tracks shared specific claims, not parallel existence).

discoverability.competitive_assessment: Day +8 post-migration. Status quo: we are indexed and producing impressions (last operator forward 2026-04-25: 23 impressions / 0 clicks / 3-month GSC, mostly URL-pattern false positives in the query mix); dome remains static (Day 12 author silence on machine-readable layer; Day 12 manual-commit drought; Day 8 of registry-tally stability at 69/94.5%). Today's 8th consecutive identical-registry daily-review regen continues a pattern that supports our 'the daily-review pipeline does what it's supposed to do — surface the registry tally — and the registry isn't changing because the author isn't iterating' read. The top_passing rotation that was mechanical yesterday (WIN-073 → WIN-026) is NOT a rotation today — Crepuscular Ray Convergence remains top_passing. NEW DATA POINT: a second AI-enabled flat-earth actor (Space Audits / aether-cosmology) is materially more publicly visible than the dome in raw web-search and platform-presence terms (16/8 stars/forks, multi-platform YouTube + X Spaces + Rumble + community forum + multiple wiki entries). The two actors are parallel and non-cross-referencing. This does not change our discoverability strategy for the dome review specifically (we are not writing about aether-cosmology), but it reframes 'AI-enabled flat-earth theorizing' as a small ecosystem rather than the dome being a singular case.

Verified again today: funwithscience-org.github.io 301 redirects work; the apex hostname's TLS endpoint produces 'unexpected eof while reading' across all four GitHub Pages anycast IPs. Dome's GitHub Pages endpoint fetches normally from the same sandbox in the same run, ruling out a global TLS/network problem in the sandbox.

no_action_gaps.reasoning: Stars/forks on a critical-review repo are not the discoverability metric we should optimize for. The cosmology-skeptic audience is researchers, journalists, fact-checkers, and curious laypeople who arrive via search, not GitHub stargazers. Optimizing for repo stars would distort our tone toward debate-bait rather than methodology. The right metric is GSC click-through-rate + Fact Check Explorer surfaces + inbound links from authoritative sources, all of which are tracked already.

no_action_gaps.gap: Aether-cosmology has multi-platform YouTube + X Spaces + Rumble + dedicated community forum + multiple wiki entries; we have zero external platforms.

When/if outreach is approved, the brandable funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ URL is materially better positioned to pitch than the legacy github.io URL was.

dome_changes_detected.change: 8th consecutive daily-review regen (062d0d31 / 050b3de batch 2026-04-29 morning UTC). generated_at advanced to 2026-04-29T07:35:48Z. registry block UNCHANGED: confirmed=69, prospective=10, refined=4, registry_ratio_pct=94.5. live_monitor: passed=28 / total_scored=28 (unchanged). domain_review.top_passing: WIN-026 'Crepuscular Ray Convergence' (predicted=5733km observed=5733km error_pct=0.0) — SAME as yesterday, no rotation today.
dome_changes_detected.assessment: Mechanical regen with no top_passing rotation. The HOLD pattern at 94.5% has now run 8 consecutive daily regens (longest in our recorded history; extends yesterday's 7-day record). Under the author-contact prior (HNOTE-001) — no responsive-classification triggered because the dome surface produced no surface change today. The repeated identical top_passing across two consecutive days is also data: yesterday's framing of 'mechanical day-to-day rotation' was wrong-by-one — the pick is presumably a top-N sort that is stable when no scoring inputs change. Adjusting prior accordingly: the rotation is NOT mechanical-by-design, it's mechanical-by-data-stability. When the registry doesn't change, the top_passing pick doesn't either.

tinker_routing.title: Sandbox-side TLS handshake failure on funwithscience.net (apex + sub-paths) — second consecutive day
tinker_routing.summary: curl from this scheduled-task sandbox to https://funwithscience.net/* produces 'TLSv1.3 (OUT), TLS alert, decode error (562)' / 'unexpected eof while reading' across all four GitHub Pages anycast IPs (185.199.108-111.153) and across all sub-paths (/, /dome-model-review/, /KEV-analysis/, /llms.txt). Connection establishes (TCP 443 open, ALPN offered, Client hello sent), then the server emits a TLS decode-error alert on the very next outbound flight. Same sandbox + same run successfully completes TLS handshakes to https://john09289.github.io/, https://api.github.com/, https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ — so the failure is hostname-specific, not sandbox-global. Workaround in use: fetch via GitHub Contents API + raw URLs; this works for verification but means we cannot test live-site rendering from the sandbox. The 301-redirect verification from the legacy URL (funwithscience-org.github.io) works fine because curl follows the redirect to the github.io-hosted endpoint, not the apex-CNAME-fronted endpoint.
tinker_routing.verified_today: TLS handshake fails on funwithscience.net apex + dome-model-review/ + KEV-analysis/. john09289.github.io fetches HTTP/2 200 normally. funwithscience-org.github.io 301s normally. Repro across HTTP/1.1 fallback, --tls-max 1.2, four anycast IP overrides, and wget — all fail identically.
tinker_routing.hypothesis: Likely a SNI / Cloudflare-fronted-vs-direct GitHub-Pages mismatch, or a CIPHER-suite negotiation issue specific to the funwithscience.net cert path. Less likely: certificate-chain issue (would expect a CERT verification error, not decode-error). Even less likely: a GitHub Pages outage (would affect github.io endpoints too).
tinker_routing.user_impact_assessment: ZERO direct user impact — operator-confirmed in prior session that the live URL works from normal browsers and from external services (GSC fetched the sitemaps successfully on 2026-04-21/23/24). This is a sandbox-environmental problem only. Recommendation: tinker logs as known-environmental-quirk in monitor/integrity/ but does not file as a review-content-side issue.
tinker_routing.operator_decision_optional: If tinker can isolate the cause (e.g., specific OpenSSL version mismatch, specific cipher suite the cowork sandbox negotiates that GitHub Pages' apex serving rejects), worth noting in the ops doc. If it's just a known sandbox quirk, log + move on.
tinker_routing.escalation_threshold: If a third consecutive day (2026-04-30 run) STILL shows the failure, file a tinker proposal explicitly. If by that point an external-side validation (operator-side curl, browser fetch, GSC re-forward) shows the live site is working fine for actual users, escalation is to log as 'known sandbox quirk' rather than 'operational issue.'

streak_counters.drought_context: Day 12 (11.94 days from a57e731 at 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z). The drought now exceeds the prior 10-day gap (2026-04-07 → 2026-04-17) by ~2 days. Per the contrarian read I recorded yesterday: this is still n=2 (one prior gap + the current gap), not a cycle, and the honest framing remains 'silence persists with multiple equally-defensible explanations.' But the registry-stability streak (8 days) IS the longest in our recorded history, and that's a separate observable from the author-silence streak.

contrarian_read.claim: Today's status counters '8 consecutive identical regens' as a meaningful milestone.
contrarian_read.counter: It's a milestone in our log, but it's not necessarily a milestone in the author's view. The dome's auto-pipeline's job IS to produce identical regens when the registry hasn't changed; from the author's side this is the system working correctly, not the author 'failing to iterate.' Our framing should not conflate 'engineering hold' with 'epistemic hold.' If the author is on vacation, distracted, satisfied with V51.1, or working on a major rewrite that hasn't landed yet, all of those produce the same observable. We don't know which.

contrarian_read.claim: Aether-cosmology being more publicly visible than the dome reframes the discoverability strategy.
contrarian_read.counter: It actually doesn't, and we should be careful not to over-update on this. Aether-cosmology has been around longer (they have a community forum at aethercosmology.com which implies multi-year buildout) and operates in a different content modality (live X Spaces + YouTube + community-driven discussion vs. dome's self-publishing-only registry). Their stars/forks/platform-presence is a function of activity-time and modality choice, not of inherent claim-value. Our review is critique-of-a-specific-model and the right discoverability axis is 'people searching for that specific model' — not 'people in the broader flat-earth ecosystem.' The dome being a small invisible target makes our review correspondingly small in audience but is the correct scope. Reframing toward broader ecosystem coverage would be scope creep against operator's explicit instruction.

contrarian_read.claim: TLS handshake failure for two consecutive days warrants escalation.
contrarian_read.counter: The failure is sandbox-side (zero impact on actual users; GSC and external services have validated the live URL works). Escalating to operator without first ruling out 'known cowork sandbox quirk' would burn operator attention on something that's likely environmental. The escalation threshold I set (third day + cross-check that external validation shows the site is live) is correct — but I should not weight the sandbox failure as evidence of a real site problem.

Sandbox-side TLS handshake to funwithscience.net failed for the second day; routed to tinker as environmental probe rather than open-issue.
